paper_title: Identification of shallot pathogens in Vĩnh Châu town of Sóc Trăng province

entities.description: The study of diseases in plants, specifically focusing on shallot pathogens.
The primary research problem involving identifying organisms causing diseases in shallots.
A disease symptom characterized by the breakdown of plant tissues, caused by Erwinia carotovora.
A disease causing decay in the bulbs of shallots.
A disease caused by Aspergillus niger, resulting in black spores on bulbs.
A fungal disease caused by Colletotrichum gloeosporioides.
A disease affecting the base of the shallot bulb, caused by Fusarium oxysporum.
Author of the research article.
Author of the research article.
Corresponding author of the research article.

entities.description: The affiliation of the researchers in Vietnam.
A set of criteria to establish a causative relationship between a microbe and a disease.
Identifying pathogens based on physical characteristics under a microscope.
Polymerase Chain Reaction used to amplify specific DNA sequences for identification.
A molecular technique used to identify bacterial species by sequencing their ribosomal RNA genes.
A culture medium used for the isolation of bacteria.
A culture medium used for the isolation of fungi.
A chemical used for surface sterilization of samples.
A public database of nucleotide sequences used for comparative analysis.
The degree of sequence identity found for P. aeruginosa identification.
The acidity level of the NA medium used.
The size of the PCR product for E. carotovora identification.
The size of the PCR product for Pseudomonas sp. identification.
The size of the PCR product for Aspergillus niger identification.
